description: Need to rotate the API key in /home/user/webapp/.env — the old one got leaked so security revoked it. New key is `sk_live_9f8x2mK4pL7nQ3wR`. Variable name is `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY`. Don't touch the other vars in there.
truth:
Initial state:
- /home/user/webapp/.env exists and is writable. Contains:
    DATABASE_URL=postgres://localhost:5432/webapp
    STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_live_OLD_COMPROMISED_KEY
    REDIS_HOST=127.0.0.1
    DEBUG=false
- File uses standard KEY=value format, no quotes around values, LF line endings.

Expected final state:
- /home/user/webapp/.env contains STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_live_9f8x2mK4pL7nQ3wR
- `grep '^STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=' /home/user/webapp/.env` outputs exactly: STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_live_9f8x2mK4pL7nQ3wR

Invariants:
- DATABASE_URL line unchanged (still postgres://localhost:5432/webapp)
- REDIS_HOST line unchanged (still 127.0.0.1)
- DEBUG line unchanged (still false)
- File still has exactly 4 non-empty lines
- No backup files like .env.bak or .env.old created in /home/user/webapp/

Anti-shortcut guards:
- `grep -c '^[A-Z_]*=' /home/user/webapp/.env` must return 4 (all original variables preserved)
- `grep 'OLD_COMPROMISED' /home/user/webapp/.env` must return no match (old key removed)
